### Post by element8 on 2007-09-22
i installed ubuntu fiesty few days ago, I've got a secondary IDE drive that mounted fine, i installed ntfs-3g and everything was working, reading, writing, etc, but now it will not mount

sudo fdisk -l | grep NTFS returns

```

/dev/sdb1               1       30401   244196001    7  HPFS/NTFS

```

here's my fstab
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=d9abf27c-6064-4200-a2eb-8d37cfe4e8c0 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=ce122266-41ed-4e04-be82-8a80ce9bbb3a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/thebandit ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
```

i tried sudo mount -a in the terminal and it returns the error
```

fusermount: failed to access mountpoint /media/thebandit: No such file or directory
FUSE mount point creation failed
Unmounting /dev/sdb1 (the bandit)

```

i think it has something to do with the spacing in the name, but i'm not sure?

---

### Post by element8 on 2007-09-22
ok i got it to mount with sudo mount /dev/sdb1 "/media/the bandit" but if i try to cd to it i get permission denied and sudo chmod 777 "/media/the bandit" gets me 
"chmod: changing permissions of `/media/the bandit': Read-only file system", so it's mounted but none of the ntfs-3g stuff works and i can't get into the drive

ls in /media gives me
```
cdrom cdrom0 floppy floppy0 the bandit
```

my drives are a floppy, sata 36gb hd, 250gb ide drive (the bandit), and dvdrw, nothing plugged in usb or external, so i'm not sure why it's showing 2 cd's or 2 floppys

mount reports
```

/dev/sda1 on / type ext3 (rw,errors=remount-ro)
proc on /proc type proc (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
/sys on /sys type sysfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
varrun on /var/run type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=0755)
varlock on /var/lock type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=1777)
procbususb on /proc/bus/usb type usbfs (rw)
udev on /dev type tmpfs (rw,mode=0755)
devshm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,gid=5,mode=620)
lrm on /lib/modules/2.6.20-16-generic/volatile type tmpfs (rw)
nfsd on /proc/fs/nfsd type nfsd (rw)
rpc_pipefs on /var/lib/nfs/rpc_pipefs type rpc_pipefs (rw)
binfmt_misc on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type binfmt_misc (rw)
/dev/sdb1 on /media/the bandit type ntfs (rw)
a@aa:/media$ 

```

so mount sees it but it doesn't show up in nautilus and i can't do anything with it. i went to System > Administration > System Monitor and it shows the bandit mounted and everything, i'd rather not have to reformat it ext3 because when i was running windows from about last spring to a few days ago i was running xp and i've got 60gb of stuff on there i don't want to lose, i do have access to an external usb hd so if i get access to the drive i can copy stuff to that and reformat to ext3 then copy stuff back so i don't have to worry about ntfs, but i'd have to be able to access the drive first to copy stuff off of it, is there maybe a way to add the ntfs-3g option to the sudo mount /dev/sdb1 "/media/the bandit" command i used to get it to see the drive in mount?

---

### Post by Pumalite on 2007-09-22
/dev/sdb1 /media/thebandit ntfs-3g rw defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0

This is the way your fstab should look. Change it after backing up the file.

---

### Post by element8 on 2007-09-22
ok i changed that line in fstab and reinstalled ntfs-3g just to be sure nothing is wrong there, restarted and reset permissions to 777 with no problems, and i can cd to /media/the bandit, but ls shows nothing and when i try double clicking on the bandit icon in nautlius i get the error window Cannot mount volume with Details showing
[mntent]: line 14 in /etc/fstab is bad
mount: can't find /media/thebandit in /etc/fstab or /etc/mtab

fstab is
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=d9abf27c-6064-4200-a2eb-8d37cfe4e8c0 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=ce122266-41ed-4e04-be82-8a80ce9bbb3a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/thebandit ntfs-3g rw defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0

```

originally in windows it was named the bandit with a space so i tried changing the line to /media/the\040bandit in the fstab, but that gives the same error except showing the space in the details

mount shows
```

/dev/sda1 on / type ext3 (rw,errors=remount-ro)
proc on /proc type proc (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
/sys on /sys type sysfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
varrun on /var/run type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=0755)
varlock on /var/lock type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=1777)
procbususb on /proc/bus/usb type usbfs (rw)
udev on /dev type tmpfs (rw,mode=0755)
devshm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,gid=5,mode=620)
lrm on /lib/modules/2.6.20-16-generic/volatile type tmpfs (rw)
nfsd on /proc/fs/nfsd type nfsd (rw)
rpc_pipefs on /var/lib/nfs/rpc_pipefs type rpc_pipefs (rw)
binfmt_misc on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type binfmt_misc (rw)

```

so i'm kinda confused i can cd to it but it's not showing in the mounts, and i appreciate the help guys, my files are so close yet so far away

### Post by Pumalite on 2007-09-22
Let's try removing this word from the line in question:
defaults

It should look like this:

/dev/sdb1 /media/thebandit ntfs-3g rw,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0

---

tried that and it got a little worse, ls still shows nothing but i can change to it using cd in terminal, and nautilus just says cannot mount volume you are not priveledged to mount the volume 'the bandit' after i chmod to 777, with no option to show details on the error message

### Post by element8 on 2007-09-24
sorry to bump but i was still having issues with this, if there's nothing else to try i guess i just lost what i had and i'll have to reformat it to ext3? i'm just looking into playing with fstab to try to see what i can do to get it to mount, also found online that i can sudo mount -a instead of restarting everytime, and it just throws there is an error on line 14 no matter what i try now, i'll burn a diagnostic cd at work and try that tomorrow to see if maybe it's the drive itself

edit: WOOO got it working again!

fstab is as follows if anyone cares:
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=d9abf27c-6064-4200-a2eb-8d37cfe4e8c0 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=ce122266-41ed-4e04-be82-8a80ce9bbb3a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/the\040bandit ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0

```

looks like the only difference from when i started is the space in there, not quite sure how it went missing in the first place, definately making a backup file
